title: Biodiversity Day: Nature's Amazing Party!
story: Every creature on Earth gets a special shout-out on May 22 during the International Day for Biological Diversity! This day celebrates all the amazing plants, animals, and tiny organisms that make our planet so incredible. People around the world share photos of cool bugs, colorful birds, and weird plants they find in their backyards. Schools organize nature walks, zoos host special events, and families go on scavenger hunts to spot different species. It's like a giant celebration of how awesome and diverse life on Earth really is!
activity_question: What do people commonly do to celebrate this day?
activity_choices: Share photos of plants and animals they find
Wear only green clothing all day
activity_answer: Share photos of plants and animals they find
follow_up_question: What's the most unusual plant or animal you've ever seen in your neighborhood?

title: Ships Ahoy for Maritime Day!
story: Every May 22, America celebrates National Maritime Day to honor the brave sailors and amazing ships that keep our oceans busy! People visit maritime museums, tour historic ships, and learn about life at sea. Some coastal cities throw festivals with boat parades and seafood feasts. It's the perfect day to appreciate how ships bring us everything from toys to bananas from faraway places!
activity_question: What do people often visit on National Maritime Day?
bonus_fact: The first steamship crossed the Atlantic Ocean in 1819, taking 29 days!
